evals.prompt: Define the UX strategy for a B2B project management tool. Business goal: increase team adoption by 40% in 12 months. Current pain: the tool is powerful but onboarding takes 3+ weeks and users feel overwhelmed. No prior UX strategy artifact exists.
evals.expected_output: A ux-strategy.md artifact committed to the resolved layout path with frontmatter type: ux-strategy. The artifact covers three layers: (1) Vision — a specific, falsifiable statement of what the end-to-end experience should achieve, aligned to the business goal of increased adoption; (2) Goals and Measures — 2–4 UX-level OKRs with measurable Key Results (e.g., task-completion rate, time-to-first-value); the value loop is named for at least one goal (how value compounds after first success); the adoption hypothesis is stated (the UX-level first-success event and what it predicts); (3) Plan — initiatives that close the gap between current experience and the Vision, sequenced and time-horizoned. The Levy four-tenets check is applied; any weak tenet is named as a gap. The document notes its upstream position relative to journey-mapping. The NN/g three-layer model, Jaime Levy four tenets, and Gothelf/Seiden OKR-linked UX framing are referenced by name. The resolved path is surfaced before writing.
assertions: Vision layer contains a specific, falsifiable aspiration aligned to the named business goal — not a generic 'great experience' statement
Goals and Measures contains 2–4 UX-level OKRs with measurable Key Results; at least one leading and one lagging indicator are named
Value loop is named for at least one Goal: the reinforcing mechanism that brings the user back after first success
Adoption hypothesis is stated: the UX-level first-success event (a specific behavioral observable) and what leading indicator it predicts
The first-success event is behavioral — not 'user completes onboarding' or 'user signs up'; it names the value the user receives
Levy four-tenets check is applied; any weak tenet (business strategy coherence, value innovation, validated user research, killer UX vision) is named as a gap
Plan layer names at least two design initiatives, each mapped to at least one Goal + Measure KR, sequenced with a time horizon
The document explicitly notes its upstream position: 'this UX strategy is upstream of journey-mapping (experience-design pack)'
NN/g three-layer model, Jaime Levy four tenets, and Gothelf/Seiden OKR-linked UX framing are named; no verbatim framework text or values table appears
The artifact is committed to the resolved path with frontmatter type: ux-strategy; path is surfaced before writing
